expectations.text: Fetches the GitHub issue with comments before slicing.
expectations.evidence: issue view <issue> --repo <owner/repo> --comments` for an existing GitHub issue. 3. Ran `gh issue view 142 --repo acme/platform --comments` after creating the assigned run directory. 4. The comman

expectations.text: Presents a numbered proposed breakdown before creating child issues because the source is an existing GitHub issue.
Includes execution-wave or equivalent ready-order guidance so future agents can tell what comes next.
Creates a queue-guide/task-graph child issue because the parent GitHub issue cannot be edited, and makes clear that the executable tasks remain sibling direct sub-issues of the PRD issue.
Queue guidance tells future agents to pick the lowest ready AFK wave/task whose blockers are closed, and to hold HITL work until the named decision or review is complete.
Attaches child issues to the parent using addSubIssue without GraphQL variables in the mutation signature.

uncertainties: Assumed `acme/platform` was the intended repository because it was provided in the eval prompt. Since `gh issue view` could not resolve that repository, I did not proceed beyond context gathering.
